item.title: One Leveraged ETF Is Reshaping Trading in World's Top AI Memory Stock
item.description: A Hong Kong fund tied to SK Hynix has grown so large that it's beginning to move the stock it was built to track.

item.title: South Korean Banks Expand Trading Floors for 24-Hour Won Market
item.description: South Korea’s biggest lenders are boosting trading desks in London and Seoul to prepare for the won’s round-the-clock trading starting next week.

item.title: 【今朝の5本】仕事を始める前に読んでおきたい厳選ニュース
item.description: メタのクラウド事業、ウォーシュ議長講演、カカクコム買収提案、米経済指標、北米貿易協定

item.title: AI Money and Anxiety Are Powering This Election Cycle
item.description: AI is sparking nervousness among voters. AI money is funding the candidates. And AI is generating the campaign ads. On today’s Big Take podcast, how the first AI election mirrors the anxieties of our era.

item.title: ByteDance construye en Brasil su mayor complejo de centros de datos fuera de China
item.description: El centro de datos de US$39.000 millones del creador de TikTok es el fruto más reciente de los esfuerzos de Lula por atraer inversión china.

item.title: Apple Readies New iPad Pro, Redesigned Entry MacBook Pro for 2027
item.description: Apple Inc. is preparing an upgraded iPad Pro line and a redesigned entry-level MacBook Pro for next year, adding to a slate of several other major new products planned for the iPhone’s 20th anniversary year.

item.title: Northern Ireland Riots Stoked by Social Media Accounts From US, UK
item.description: Researchers found that 30% of accounts that amplified inflammatory statements in days after knife attack were based in the US.

item.title: M&A Volume to Stay Strong in Second Half, Says Sullivan’s Sawyer
item.description: The variety, range and scope of deals in the market suggests transaction volumes will continue to remain strong in the second half, according to Melissa Sawyer, head of global mergers and acquisitions at Sullivan & Cromwell.

item.title: Musk Ally Gracias Eyes Bets in AI, Energy After SpaceX Windfall
item.description: Antonio Gracias, a long-time SpaceX board member, is looking for more investments in artificial intelligence, energy and space after bets in Elon Musk’s company netted his fund nearly $100 billion.

item.title: The SEC Doesn’t Want to Be in the Biz of Picking ETF Winners
item.description: The US regulator is rethinking how it oversees the ETF industry

item.title: Apple Seeks to Buy Chinese-Made Memory Chips With Lobbying Push
item.description: Apple Inc. is in negotiations to purchase chips from two Chinese semiconductor makers on a Pentagon blacklist to help reduce the impact of a global memory shortage that’s forced the company to raise prices across its product line.

item.title: Musk Meme Traders Rush Back Into Tesla When the Stock Hits $420
item.description: Crossing $420 gets Tesla Inc. investors excited.

item.title: Michael Burry’s Short Call Dents Caterpillar’s AI-Fueled Rally
item.description: Caterpillar Inc. stock slid from a record high Wednesday after Michael Burry, made famous in The Big Short for his bets against the US housing market ahead of the 2008 crisis, said he shorted the industrial heavyweight for the first time.

item.title: Big Bank Calls Unleash Trading Frenzy in US Funding Rate Futures
item.description: US short-term interest-rate markets are seeing a surge of activity thanks to top Wall Street banks peddling divergent views on how an expected increase in the supply of Treasury bills this month is likely to play out.

item.title: Venezuelan Private Sector Fills State Void in Earthquake Relief
item.description: After years of treating entrepreneurs as adversaries — subjecting them to strict regulations and threats of expropriation — Venezuela’s socialist government is now turning to the private sector in the aftermath of devastating twin earthquakes that have so far left nearly 2,000 dead and 15,000 displaced.

item.title: Tankmaker KNDS Postpones Its IPO Citing Market Conditions
item.description: Tankmaker KNDS intends to resume its initial public offering process when market conditions improve, according to a Wednesday statement, postponing what was set to be one of Europe’s largest debuts in recent years.

item.title: New Zealand House Prices Near Three-Year Low on Iran War Worries
item.description: New Zealand house prices fell for the third straight month in June, nearing a three-year low and adding to signs of an economic slowdown as the global oil shock hits household incomes and consumer confidence.

item.title: Alibaba, Payment Firm Will Pay $600 Million to Resolve US Probe
item.description: Chinese tech giant Alibaba Group Holding Ltd and its US digital payment processor agreed to pay $600 million to resolve a federal investigation of whether they failed to prevent the sale and importation of illegal pharmaceuticals and controlled substances, the Justice Department said.

item.title: Fed’s Warsh Picks Senior Bessent Staffer to Serve as Adviser
item.description: A senior aide to Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent is set to join the Federal Reserve as an adviser to Chairman Kevin Warsh, according to two people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Shell Shuffles Energy Trading Bosses as David Wells Retires
item.description: Shell Plc Executive Vice President of energy trading David Wells is retiring after four years of running a unit that’s a key driver for the company’s earnings, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Robinhood Rolls Out Perpetual Futures Tied to Commodities, ETFs
item.description: Robinhood Markets Inc. is rolling out perpetual futures tied to commodities, exchange-traded funds and currencies in Europe, seeking to capitalize on the rise in round-the-clock trading that’s long been a feature of crypto markets.

item.title: Wall Street Worries Less Fed Talk Will Spur Market Volatility
item.description: It’s only been a few weeks since Kevin Warsh took the helm of the Federal Reserve, but analysts are already concerned that one of his key promises — a quieter central bank — will lead to more volatility in the market.

item.title: ECB Views Splinter on Next Rate Move as Inflation Sinks With Oil
item.description: Just three weeks after the European Central Bank unanimously raised interest rates in response to the Iran war, there’s no consensus on what to do next.

item.title: Exxon to Change Name for First Time in Decades After Redomicile
item.description: Exxon Mobil Corp. will begin trading as ExxonMobil Holdings Corp. after redomiciling in Texas, the first name change since the Mobil mega merger more than 25 years ago.

item.title: US Crude Grades Shed Iran War Premium as Energy Crisis Eases
item.description: Key US oil grades have slumped to trade at a discount again as a gush of crude flows through the Strait of Hormuz and a recent spike in demand for American exports fades.

item.title: Finance App Network Maker Plaid Is Said to Consider US IPO
item.description: Plaid Inc., a technology company whose network helps fintech apps connect to customer accounts, is considering an initial public offering, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: ECB’s Stournaras Sees Smaller Likelihood of Further Rate Hike
item.description: The unexpectedly large fall in energy prices alongside slowing euro-zone inflation suggest the European Central Bank may not have to add to June’s interest-rate increase, Governing Council member Yannis Stournaras said.

item.title: BlackRock’s Tseng to Exit as CEO of Troubled Private Credit Fund
item.description: The head of BlackRock Inc.’s beleaguered private credit fund is in the process of leaving the firm, a move that follows months of losses on soured loans and revelations of a US regulatory probe into the unit’s valuation practices.

item.title: Russian Gasoline Prices Rise Again After Ukraine Hits Refineries
item.description: Retail fuel prices in Russia notched up another significant increase as recent Ukrainian drone strikes on the country’s oil refineries have led to a nationwide gasoline shortage, long lines at pumping stations and renewed concerns about accelerating inflation.

item.title: Hungarian Mayor Tells Battery Maker to Leave Over Pollution Case
item.description: The mayor of Hungary’s second-largest city called on Chinese battery-parts maker Semcorp to walk away from a new, 65 billion-forint ($210 million) factory that’s been closed for alleged environmental violations.
